Job Role Description: Case Managers provide individualised assessment, planning, coordination, and direct support or therapeutic intervention to program participants. They work closely with individuals to identify their needs, set goals, navigate support systems, and facilitate positive outcomes, upholding professional standards and confidentiality. Key Responsibilities: Conduct comprehensive assessments of client needs, strengths, and goals. Develop, implement, and review individualised support plans or case management plans. Provide advocacy, information, and referral services to connect clients with appropriate internal and external supports. Deliver direct counselling or therapeutic interventions where qualified and appropriate. Monitor client progress, conduct regular reviews, and adjust plans as needed. Maintain accurate, confidential, and up-to-date client records in accordance with privacy legislation and organisational policy. Collaborate with frontline staff, program coordinators, and other stakeholders to ensure holistic client support. Adhere to professional ethical guidelines, confidentiality principles, and relevant legislative requirements. Manage a caseload effectively and efficiently.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Essential: Tertiary qualification in Social Work, Psychology, Counselling, Community Services, or a related field (minimum Bachelor's degree for professional roles). Minimum of 2-5 years of experience in case management, counselling, or direct client support, preferably within the not-for-profit or community sector in Australia. Demonstrated experience in assessment, care planning, and goal setting. Strong understanding of relevant Australian legislation (e.g., privacy, child safety, mental health acts). Excellent interpersonal, empathy, and active listening skills. Ability to work autonomously and manage a caseload effectively. Current Working With Children Check (WWCC) and National Police Check (or ability to obtain). Desirable: Membership with a relevant professional body (e.g., AASW, PACFA). Experience with specific client management systems (CRM). Trauma-informed practice training.

Job Role Description: This position holds Authorisations and Delegations Purchasing Authority Level: $10,000

The Primary Purpose of the Role As the Pharmacy Administrator for YUDC Limited, your primary purpose is to provide efficient administrative, communication, and coordination support to ensure the smooth operation of the pharmacy and related pharmacy functions. You will act as the central communication hub, managing client and internal team correspondence across multiple platforms and coordinating administrative workflows to allow the pharmacy team to focus solely on clinical and dispensing duties. By maintaining clear communication, accurate documentation, and organised administrative processes, you will play a key role in supporting client satisfaction, operational efficiency, and regulatory compliance within the pharmacy. Our Purpose, Vision and Core Values Green Leaf Solutions is embarking on a groundbreaking mission to revolutionise healthcare. From redefining the essence of health to reimagining the significance of care in healthcare, we're committed to reshaping the experience of both our clients and our team members. We achieve this by upholding our values of: Genuine Commitment to Care Audacious attitude Nurture ownership Jump at opportunities for growth Altruistic Collaboration Key Responsibilities First Line of Communication: Monitor and manage all incoming pharmacy communications, including team messages, phone calls, and emails, ensuring timely and professional responses. Manage Intercom communications, including responding to customer queries, and creating tickets or assigning messages to appropriate staff where required; and within SLAs. Coordinate internal communications between the pharmacy, logistics, and client services teams to ensure consistent and accurate information flow. Customer Service and Support: Provide compassionate and efficient support to clients across various communication channels, including phone (inbound and outbound), email, and in-person interactions. Own and manage all customer order and payment systems, including Shopify, Windcave, AfterPay, ZIP and EFTPOS. Accurately process and reconcile client orders placed online, over the phone, and in person, ensuring all transaction details are recorded, verified, and integrated across the relevant platforms. Maintain responsibility for the accuracy, functionality, and daily performance of these systems to ensure smooth, compliant, and timely processing of every client purchase. Support the pharmacy team by managing administrative aspects of client interactions to enhance overall service delivery. Provide professional, friendly, and efficient prescription pickup (Click and Collect) and customer service support to clients, if and when required. Accounts Documentation: Ensure all packing slips received from the Logistics Team are promptly scanned and forwarded to the Accounts Team for processing and reconciliation. Respond to and resolve accounts-related queries, coordinating with relevant departments to ensure accuracy and clarity. Maintain organised digital and physical records of purchase orders, invoices, and communication notes in tracking spreadsheet. Administrative Support and Coordination: Provide general administrative assistance to the Pharmacy Team, Product Strategy Manager, and other senior leadership ensuring seamless coordination of day-to-day activities. Maintain the cleanliness and organisation of the reception space daily, including waiting areas, to create a welcoming environment for clients and staff. Assist in coordinating team workflows and priorities, ensuring tasks are completed efficiently and within deadlines. Assist with office management tasks such as ordering supplies, coordinating maintenance and repairs, and overseeing facility operations. Assist with additional tasks such as putting out bins, dismantling boxes for recycling, and other office maintenance duties as required. Operational Efficiency: Identify administrative process improvements to enhance communication flow and efficiency. Ensure high standards of professionalism, confidentiality, and accuracy in all communications and administrative functions. Support management in implementing new systems or tools that improve administrative coordination and client experience. Compliance and Confidentiality: Maintain strict confidentiality of all client, supplier, and company information. Ensure compliance with industry regulations, standards, and internal policies to mitigate risks and maintain integrity across operations. Follow established Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for all administrative and communication tasks. Communication and Collaboration: Facilitate effective communication within and between teams and business entities to enhance service delivery and client care. Participate in team meetings, providing updates and feedback from client interactions. Work collaboratively with team members to achieve business goals and improve client service strategies. Health, Safety and Wellbeing: Comply with all company policies, standards and procedures. Demonstrates employee health, safety and wellbeing is a high priority by incorporating health, safety and wellbeing into regular formal and informal group and individual meetings. Be familiar with emergency and evacuation procedures. Report all incidents, near misses and injuries immediately. Actively cooperate with any rehabilitation programme that is arranged to assist recovery from injury for yourself or for fellow employees. Vision, Values, and Organisational Culture: Uphold the company values and demonstrate these within your work. Participate as an active citizen within YUDC Limited by playing an active role in culture, team events, and daily organisational life. General Duties: Be punctual and work the hours and times specified. Prioritise workload to ensure work of the greatest importance to the business is undertaken with urgency and to a high standard. Support and help develop a positive workplace culture. Demonstrate excellent interpersonal communication skills. Responsibly manage all business resources within accountability levels. Undertake all duties and responsibilities outlined in this job description and all other duties as required by the business. Comply with all employment obligations. Promptly undertake to complete all reasonable and lawful instructions and directions given. Serve the business in good faith, promoting and protecting the business's best interests. During work time, and such other times as may be reasonably required, dedicate all effort to the execution and fulfillment of the duties, responsibilities, obligations, and instructions related to employment. Demonstrate through own actions a commitment to Health and Safety at work when undertaking work or observing others in the workplace.
Job Role Skills and Experience: NZ Pharmacy technician qualification level 4 or above. Prior experience (2+ years) in administrative support, preferably within a pharmacy, healthcare, or logistics environment. Exceptional communication skills, both written and phone. High attention to detail and accuracy. Demonstrated proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Excel, Word) and familiarity with Shopify, Intercom, or similar communication and order management systems. Job Role Title: Aboriginal Family Lead Decision Making Convener
Job Role Description: The Position Reporting to the Program Manager the AFLDM Convenor will hold a key role in developing the procedures for the family meeting processes. The role will convene family meetings as well as engaging families, finding and activating family support networks as well as negotiating with families where there are significant concerns for the safety and wellbeing of children and young people. This position will require some travel and after hours work. Key Responsibilities Lead the development of the Family Meeting practice and procedures. Actively seek feedback from children, young people, their families and community on ways to improve the processes and program. Schedule meetings in line with process timelines. Ensure all significant people are personally invited and have ability to participate in the meetings. Facilitate meetings in cultural ways that ensure all participants have a voice. Ensure accurate record of meetings are taken and recorded. Skilfully manage conflict to negotiate common goals. Where needed, work with families to develop Case Plans that address the concerns and promote the child or young persons best interests. Endorse decisions and recommendations in- line with policy and procedures. Where the need is identified, research extended family and work to activate them as support to the children and families. Actively engage with families identifying strengths and capacities, highlighting these in family meetings.
Job Role Identifier: Aboriginal Family Lead Decision Making Convener

Job Role Description: POSITION SUMMARY The West Coast Utilities Manager is responsible for the successful management, delivery, and performance of water, wastewater, and stormwater utilities contracts across Westroads West Coast clients. This role oversees operational, commercial, financial, and contractual obligations to ensure services are delivered safely, efficiently, and in accordance with client expectations and regulatory requirements. The West Coast Utilities Manager acts as the primary interface between the organisation and the client, leading operational teams, managing contract performance, and driving continuous improvement initiatives. The role is accountable for achieving contractual KPIs, maintaining compliance, and ensuring the long-term profitability and sustainability of utility service contracts. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES CONTRACT MANAGEMENT Manage the day-to-day delivery of water, wastewater, and stormwater service contracts. Ensure all contractual obligations, service levels, and key performance indicators (KPIs) are achieved. Monitor contract performance and implement corrective actions where required. Administer contract variations, extensions, claims, and change management processes. Prepare and present contract performance reports to clients and senior management. Identify opportunities for contract growth and additional service delivery. OPERATIONAL LEADERSHIP Lead and support operational teams responsible for maintenance, repairs, renewals, and capital works. Ensure resources, equipment, and subcontractors are effectively managed to meet service delivery requirements. Monitor operational performance, productivity, and service quality. Develop and implement operational plans that align with client and business objectives. Promote a culture of excellence, accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Lead contract performance and response to emergencies or crises. FINANCIAL & COMMERCIAL MANAGEMENT Prepare and manage contract budgets, forecasts, and financial reporting. Monitor revenue, expenditure, profitability, and contract margins. Identify and implement cost-saving opportunities while maintaining service quality. Manage procurement activities and subcontractor agreements. Ensure accurate valuation, invoicing, and payment processes. Support tender submissions and contract negotiations. HEALTH, SAFETY, ENVIRONMENT & QUALITY (HSEQ) Promote and maintain a strong health, safety, environmental, and quality culture. Ensure compliance with all legislative, regulatory, and company requirements. Lead incident investigations and implement corrective actions. Monitor environmental compliance and sustainable operational practices. Ensure quality management systems are maintained and continuously improved. REGULATORY & COMPLIANCE MANAGEMENT Ensure compliance with drinking water, wastewater, and environmental regulations. Monitor changes in legislation and industry standards. Support audits, inspections, and regulatory reporting requirements. Maintain accurate operational and compliance records. ASSET MANAGEMENT & CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT Support clients in asset management planning. Analyse operational data and performance trends to identify improvement opportunities. Implement innovations, technologies, and process improvements that enhance service delivery. Ensure emergency response plans are maintained and regularly tested. CLIENT RELATIONSHIP MANAGEMENT Serve as the primary point of contact for clients, local authorities, and stakeholders. Build and maintain strong working relationships with client representatives. Lead regular contract review meetings and performance discussions. Manage client expectations and proactively address service delivery issues. Support business development initiatives and contract retention activities. Actively seek opportunities for additional contract scope to assist with. LEADERSHIP & PEOPLE MANAGEMENT Lead, mentor, and develop operational managers, supervisors, and support staff. Conduct performance reviews and support professional development initiatives. Assist with workforce planning, recruitment, and succession planning. Foster a positive and high-performing team culture.
Job Role Skills and Experience: EDUCATION Degree or Diploma in Civil Engineering, Environmental Engineering, Construction Management, Infrastructure Management, or a related discipline. Relevant industry certifications and professional memberships are desirable. EXPERIENCE Minimum 7–10 years' experience within the water, wastewater, utilities, roading or infrastructure sector. Proven experience managing service, maintenance, or capital works contracts. Strong understanding of utility operations, asset management, and infrastructure delivery. Experience managing client relationships, commercial performance, and operational teams. Demonstrated success in achieving contractual and financial performance targets. TECHNICAL KNOWLEDGE Water distribution networks Wastewater collection systems Stormwater infrastructure Utility maintenance and renewals Contract administration and commercial management Regulatory compliance and reporting Health, Safety, Environment and Quality (HSEQ) systems Infrastructure operations and maintenance practices SKILLS & COMPETENCIES Strong leadership and people management skills. Excellent client relationship and stakeholder engagement abilities. Excellent client relationship and stakeholder engagement abilities. Effective negotiation and conflict resolution skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Ability to problem solve and manage multiple priorities in a dynamic operational environment. Sound decision-making and risk management skills.

Job Role Description: Faith Basis of Appointment We see the appointment of staff to Trinity Christian School as a ministry calling. The ethos, aims and objects of a Christian School are based on: > acceptance of the Lordship of Christ; and > acceptance of the Bible as the revealed Word of God Within Trinity Christian School these are expounded in the Doctrinal Basis. The Doctrinal Basis does not exhaust the extent of the beliefs of the School and its members. The Bible itself, as the inspired and infallible Word of God, that speaks with final authority concerning truth, morality, and the proper conduct of mankind, is the sole and final source of all that the School and its members believe. You must support the School in these areas and set an example of personal conduct and behaviour consistent with the School’s beliefs and ethos as outlined in documents made available to prospective employees prior to interview. Any appointment is conditional upon staff maintaining and demonstrating a continuing firm personal belief and lifestyle consistent with the School’s Doctrinal Basis and the other documents provided to staff, this being an essential condition of the appointment. A Learning Centred Christian School At Trinity Christian School we endeavour to make the link between our faith, what we do and how we teach. We call our students and school community to grow “loving hearts, enquiring minds and generous spirits”. At Trinity, how we teach must help our students in becoming who they were created to be in Christ. How we teach should also bring a Kingdom perspective, set students’ minds on things above, deliver meaning and show purpose. The pedagogy in our classrooms at Trinity should engage enquiring minds, build questioning, allow for grappling, exploring and reasoning. The students we teach should be given opportunities to grow and serve, so they may see who they are becoming and their purpose. As we undertake to grow our learners in both faith and learning, we must build in them the capacity to do so. As stated in our Strategic Priorities for 2024 – 2027, we have several key areas of focus that we are working toward: Christian Faith and Growth The spiritual nurturing of all individuals in the Christian faith, within a caring community of students, staff and families united by vision, mission and values. A passionate focus on realising the transformative power of the Gospel of Jesus, developing Biblical knowledge, and understanding and fulfilling the purpose of a Christian life. Excellence in Education and Learning All students actively engaged, growing and thriving as learners in a learning centred school. Parent partnership and student agency enhanced and supported. A commitment to educational excellence with the creation and support of new ways of learning, varied student pathways and models of education, to meet the needs of each learner. Community and Belonging A movement by all students, staff and families from joining and attending the school to belonging and actively investing in the Trinity community. Connection and relationship developed and strengthened with those in the School’s history as well as future community members. Connecting with the World and Shaping Tomorrow Ongoing design and formation of our 2030 learning environment for the community now and in the future. A growth in our reach and impact on Christian Education nationally and globally, including shaping a reputation as a centre for staffing development and excellence. Benefits of Working at Trinity Christian School Trinity Christian School is an employer that promotes staff excellence and values the benefit that our professional team bring to the school. In addition to the highly competitive remuneration and conditions as set out in the MEA we offer the following additional benefits to our staff across the school including: > Attractive salary packaging arrangements. > School fee concessions. > Generous annual Staff Development Award program. > Access to an Employee Assistance Program. > Access to annual influenza vaccinations. Other Essential Requirements Employment Conditions Employment will be subject to the conditions of the current ACT Christian School General Staff Multi-Enterprise Agreement 2021 or its replacement. The salary and classification for this position will be dependent on the qualifications and experience of the successful applicant. A Committed, Practicing Christian Applicants must be able to demonstrate a personal faith and commitment to the Lord Jesus Christ and a lifestyle consistent with that faith, including an active commitment to a Christian fellowship. Right to Work In Australia Applicants must be Australian Citizens or hold a current and valid working visa. The School will not consider sponsorship nor support a visa application process. Please do not submit an application if you cannot meet this requirement. Working With Vulnerable People Check In keeping with the School’s policy and legislative requirements, any offer of employment is subject to you providing a satisfactory Working With Vulnerable People check prior to commencing employment and maintaining this for the duration of your employment. Acknowledgement The requirements of this job description are intended to describe the general nature and responsibility of the work in this role. These statements are not to be construed as an exhaustive list of all duties, tasks and skills required of the job. Employees will also be required to follow any other job-related instructions and school policies, and to perform other job-related duties requested by their Manager to support the School’s compliance with its legislative obligations. The Manager may, through consultation with the employee, vary the responsibilities of the position temporarily as required, but within the skills and responsibility levels appropriate to the position. Further Information All enquiries will be treated confidentially. Initial questions can be directed to the People & Culture Administrator, Katherine Boyd by phone on +61 2 6210 7500 or email employment@tcs.act.edu.au Applications are ongoing and are to be submitted electronically as a single PDF document as an attachment to the online application form. Trinity Christian School 34 McBryde Crescent Wanniassa ACT Australia 2903

AICD Contribution • Contribute to the development of the department operation plan, suggesting ideas that create solutions for the team, and in turn the organisation. • Undertake or contribute to specific National and State projects or initiatives that support AICD’s strategic plan as required. • Proactively contribute feedback and ideas to AICD for continuous improvement and to achieve OKRs, budgets and strategic goals. Other duties in line with competencies and skills may be required on an ad hoc or permanent basis

Job Role Description: The Construction Estimator plays a critical role in ensuring the successful completion of projects by accurately forecasting costs and budgets. This position involves a combination of technical skills, analytical abilities, and effective communication to deliver precise estimates that guide project planning and execution. Key responsibilities include: Analysing project drawings and specifications to prepare detailed quantity take-offs. Researching and assessing material and labour costs to develop comprehensive cost estimates. Collaborating with architects, engineers, and other stakeholders to gain insights into project requirements. Preparing bid proposals and presenting findings to management for review. Maintaining accurate records of estimates, budgets, and actual expenses for future reference. Monitoring market trends in construction materials and labour rates to ensure competitive pricing. Assisting in the negotiation process with subcontractors and suppliers to optimise costs. This position requires keen attention to detail as well as strong organisational skills to manage multiple projects simultaneously. The Construction Estimator must also possess excellent communication skills to effectively liaise with various teams throughout the project lifecycle.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Required skills Proficient in construction estimating software and tools, such as PlanSwift or Bluebeam. Strong analytical and mathematical skills to accurately assess costs and budgets. Excellent attention to detail to ensure accuracy in estimates and project specifications. Effective communication skills for liaising with clients, contractors, and team members. Ability to read and interpret blueprints, drawings, and technical documents. Knowledge of building codes, regulations, and industry standards. Required experiences A minimum of 3 years' experience in construction estimating or a related field. Proven track record of successfully managing project estimates from start to finish. Experience working on diverse types of construction projects, including residential, commercial, and industrial sectors. Previous involvement in budgeting and cost management processes throughout the lifecycle of a project. Familiarity with procurement processes and contract negotiations within the construction industry.

Job Role Description: Purpose As the Client Services Manager at Green Leaf Solutions, your primary purpose is to lead and manage the Client Services Administration shared services team to ensure exceptional service delivery. Your responsibilities will include developing and implementing service strategies, training and mentoring team members, and continuously improving service processes to meet and exceed client expectations. By fostering a client-centric culture and leveraging your leadership skills, you will play a crucial role in enhancing client experiences, driving operational excellence, and supporting Green Leaf Solutions’ mission of revolutionising healthcare. Our Purpose, Vision and Core Values Green Leaf Solutions is embarking on a groundbreaking mission to revolutionise healthcare. From redefining the essence of health to reimagining the significance of care in healthcare, we're committed to reshaping the experience of both our clients and our team members. We achieve this by upholding our values of: Genuine Commitment to Care Audacious attitude Nurture ownership Jump at opportunities for growth Altruistic Collaboration Specific Duties and Responsibilities Strategic Planning and Execution: Develop, refine, and implement operational strategies aligned with the group’s overall objectives and long-term vision. Collaborate with senior management across the Green Leaf Group entities to establish key performance indicators (KPIs) and metrics to track progress and measure success. Team Leadership and Management: Lead the client services team towards excellence by exemplifying the group’s core values in all aspects of work. Conduct regular performance evaluations and provide training and development opportunities for team members. Conduct monthly team meetings to communicate goals, track progress, and provide guidance. Foster a culture of continuous improvement, focusing on accuracy, simplification, control, automation, efficiency, and collaboration. Guidance on Training and Development: Ensure that comprehensive training programs are developed and implemented, covering areas like product knowledge, client communication, and regulatory updates. Foster a culture of ongoing learning and development, identifying opportunities for team training and professional growth. Provide regular feedback, support, mentoring and coaching opportunities for direct reports and monitor their progress and contribution. Evaluate the effectiveness of training initiatives, ensuring they meet the evolving needs of the team and the clinic. Monitoring Performance and Reporting: Define and direct the establishment and tracking of appropriate team KPIs related to client satisfaction, administration service efficiency, and overall team performance. Analyse reports and data from the team to assess performance and identify areas for strategic improvement. Oversight of Policies, SOPs and Compliance: Ensure client service processes and SOPs are constantly questioned, improved and all unnecessary steps are removed to constantly revolutionise the client journey and experience. Keep in mind that elegant and revolutionary client service is the main goal, not one which is laborious, complicated and inefficient. Ensure consistent and legally compliant client communication by developing scripts and guidelines for client interactions, delineating what information can and cannot be shared by the team. Ensure client service processes and SOPs comply with all regulatory requirements and regularly review team compliance measures, coordinating with other departments as required. Budget Management and Resource Planning: Oversee the management of the client services budget, ensuring resources are allocated effectively to meet operational needs. Manage fixed and variable resources within the client services team, including having the right people at the right time, with the right budget. Provide strategic insight into staffing requirements and resource allocation. Recruit new team members as and when required, with the support from the People & Culture team. Process Improvement: Identify and implement process improvements to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and internal controls within the client services team. Leverage technology and automation to streamline workflows and reduce manual tasks. Communication and Collaboration: Facilitate effective communication within and between departments to enhance service delivery and client care. Participate in team meetings, providing updates and feedback from client interactions. Work collaboratively with team members to achieve business goals and improve client service strategies. Health, Safety and Wellbeing: Comply with all the group’s policies, standards and procedures. Demonstrates employee health, safety and wellbeing is a high priority by incorporating health, safety and wellbeing into regular formal and informal group and individual meetings. Be familiar with emergency and evacuation procedures. Report all incidents, near misses and injuries immediately. Actively cooperate with any rehabilitation programme that is arranged to assist recovery from injury for yourself or for fellow employees. Shared Services: Operate your role and duties across multiple entities as part of a shared services unit which provides support to a range of stakeholders. Maintain a working knowledge of the different entities and their core business to ensure your service level is appropriate to their needs. Maintain positive working relationships with stakeholders across the different entities, ensuring that you understand their working style. Operate as a service delivery superstar, acting as an (subject matter expert) for your stakeholders and ensuring high trust relationships. Vision, Values, and Organisational Culture: Uphold the Green Leaf Group values and demonstrate these within your work. Participate as an active citizen within Green Leaf Group by playing an active role in culture, team events, and daily organisational life. General Duties: Be punctual and work the hours and times specified. Prioritise workload to ensure work of the greatest importance to the business is undertaken with urgency and to a high standard. The Administration Officer and Bus Driver is responsible for providing administration support to relieve the Centre Manager and provide welfare to and transport ships’ crews from ports to Seafarers’ Centre, medical centres, airport and accommodation places. The role will assist the Centre Manager where necessary. Key Responsibilities Administration • Provide administration support to the Centre when the Centre Manager is on leave, by: o Ordering stock o Filling and maintaining levels in vending machines at Terminal o Invoicing o Taking stock control of the Centre shop Bus Maintenance • Complete daily checklist prior to leaving Centre • Ensure all relevant paperwork is contained in drivers’ folders and taken on trip inclusive of any Border Force documentation • Complete all relevant paperwork correctly • Clean buses on completion of each trip • Regularly wash exterior of buses • Ensure buses are refuelled when required using fuel card located on each bus Operational • Abide by Queensland Transport regulations in regard to transportation of passengers on buses • Abide by Stella Maris Seafarers’ Centre and Queensland Transport policies of Zero Blood Alcohol, Drug and Fatigue Management • Follow Queensland Transport speed and road rules • Assist in running of Centre including: o Maintenance of Centre o Money exchange o Centre cleaning in accordance with Queensland Health protocols o Emptying rubbish bins around the Centre regularly o Placing bins out on footpath for collection o Cleaning centre toilets regularly o Checking bar and shop stocks and advising when reordering is needed • Promote usage of the Centre, in particular to ships’ crew in relation to Foreign Exchange, shop, phone card and other facilities offered at the Centre • Report repairs that are required around the Centre to the Centre Manager • Ensure tills (Foreign Exchange, Bar and Shop) are counted prior to completing shift • Report any incidents, accidents, and damage to Centre Manager and in his absence the Chair/President of the Management Committee
